# Psalms 78

## Verses (72)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Give ear, O my people, to my law; let your ears be bent down to the words of my mouth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Opening my mouth I will give out a story, even the dark sayings of old times; | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | Which have come to our hearing and our knowledge, as they were given to us by our fathers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | We will not keep them secret from our children; we will make clear to the coming generation the praises of the Lord and his strength, and the great works of wonder which he has done. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | He put up a witness in Jacob, and made a law in Israel; which he gave to our fathers so that they might give knowledge of them to their children; | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | So that the generation to come might have knowledge of them, even the children of the future, who would give word of them to their children; | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | So that they might put their hope in God, and not let God's works go out of their minds, but keep his laws; | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | And not be like their fathers, a stiff-necked and uncontrolled generation; a generation whose heart was hard, whose spirit was not true to God. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | The children of Ephraim, armed with bows, were turned back on the day of the fight. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | They were not ruled by God's word, and they would not go in the way of his law; | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | They let his works go out of their memory, and the wonders which he had made them see. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | He did great works before the eyes of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the fields of Zoan. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | The sea was cut in two so that they might go through; the waters were massed together on this side and on that. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | In the daytime he was guiding them in the cloud, and all through the night with a light of fire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | The rocks of the waste land were broken by his power, and he gave them drink as out of the deep waters. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | He made streams come out of the rock; and waters came flowing down like rivers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | And they went on sinning against him even more, turning away from the Most High in the waste land; | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Testing God in their hearts, requesting meat for their desire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | They said bitter words against God, saying, Is God able to make ready a table in the waste land? | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | See, the rock was cut open by his power, so that the water came rushing out, and overflowing streams; is he able to give us bread? is he able to get meat for his people? | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | So these things came to the Lord's ears, and he was angry; and a fire was lighted against Jacob, and wrath came up against Israel; | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Because they had no faith in God, and no hope in his salvation. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | And he gave orders to the clouds on high, and the doors of heaven were open; | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | And he sent down manna like rain for their food, and gave them the grain of heaven. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | Man took part in the food of strong ones; he sent them meat in full measure. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | He sent an east wind from heaven, driving on the south wind by his power. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | He sent down meat on them like dust, and feathered birds like the sand of the sea, | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | And he let it come down into their resting-place, round about their tents. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | So they had food and were full; for he gave them their desire; | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | But they were not turned from their desires; and while the food was still in their mouths, | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
| [31](BBE.md#v31) | The wrath of God came on them, and put to death the fattest of them, and put an end to the young men of Israel. | [WEB](WEB.md#v31) · [KJV](KJV.md#v31) · [ASV](ASV.md#v31) |
| [32](BBE.md#v32) | For all this they went on sinning even more, and had no faith in his great wonders. | [WEB](WEB.md#v32) · [KJV](KJV.md#v32) · [ASV](ASV.md#v32) |
| [33](BBE.md#v33) | So their days were wasted like a breath, and their years in trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v33) · [KJV](KJV.md#v33) · [ASV](ASV.md#v33) |
| [34](BBE.md#v34) | When he sent death on them, then they made search for him; turning to him and looking for him with care; | [WEB](WEB.md#v34) · [KJV](KJV.md#v34) · [ASV](ASV.md#v34) |
| [35](BBE.md#v35) | In the memory that God was their Rock, and the Most High God their saviour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v35) · [KJV](KJV.md#v35) · [ASV](ASV.md#v35) |
| [36](BBE.md#v36) | But their lips were false to him, and their tongues were untrue to him; | [WEB](WEB.md#v36) · [KJV](KJV.md#v36) · [ASV](ASV.md#v36) |
| [37](BBE.md#v37) | And their hearts were not right with him, and they did not keep their agreement with him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v37) · [KJV](KJV.md#v37) · [ASV](ASV.md#v37) |
| [38](BBE.md#v38) | But he, being full of pity, has forgiveness for sin, and does not put an end to man: frequently turning back his wrath, and not being violently angry. | [WEB](WEB.md#v38) · [KJV](KJV.md#v38) · [ASV](ASV.md#v38) |
| [39](BBE.md#v39) | So he kept in mind that they were only flesh; a breath which is quickly gone, and will not come again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v39) · [KJV](KJV.md#v39) · [ASV](ASV.md#v39) |
| [40](BBE.md#v40) | How frequently did they go against him in the waste land, and give him cause for grief in the dry places! | [WEB](WEB.md#v40) · [KJV](KJV.md#v40) · [ASV](ASV.md#v40) |
| [41](BBE.md#v41) | Again they put God to the test, and gave pain to the Holy One of Israel. | [WEB](WEB.md#v41) · [KJV](KJV.md#v41) · [ASV](ASV.md#v41) |
| [42](BBE.md#v42) | They did not keep in mind the work of his hand, or the day when he took them from the power of their haters; | [WEB](WEB.md#v42) · [KJV](KJV.md#v42) · [ASV](ASV.md#v42) |
| [43](BBE.md#v43) | How he had done his signs in Egypt, and his wonders in the field of Zoan; | [WEB](WEB.md#v43) · [KJV](KJV.md#v43) · [ASV](ASV.md#v43) |
| [44](BBE.md#v44) | So that their rivers were turned to blood, and they were not able to get drink from their streams. | [WEB](WEB.md#v44) · [KJV](KJV.md#v44) · [ASV](ASV.md#v44) |
| [45](BBE.md#v45) | He sent different sorts of flies among them, poisoning their flesh; and frogs for their destruction. | [WEB](WEB.md#v45) · [KJV](KJV.md#v45) · [ASV](ASV.md#v45) |
| [46](BBE.md#v46) | He gave the increase of their fields to worms, the fruits of their industry to the locusts. | [WEB](WEB.md#v46) · [KJV](KJV.md#v46) · [ASV](ASV.md#v46) |
| [47](BBE.md#v47) | He sent ice for the destruction of their vines; their trees were damaged by the bitter cold. | [WEB](WEB.md#v47) · [KJV](KJV.md#v47) · [ASV](ASV.md#v47) |
| [48](BBE.md#v48) | Ice was rained down on their cattle; thunderstorms sent destruction among the flocks. | [WEB](WEB.md#v48) · [KJV](KJV.md#v48) · [ASV](ASV.md#v48) |
| [49](BBE.md#v49) | He sent on them the heat of his wrath, his bitter disgust, letting loose evil angels among them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v49) · [KJV](KJV.md#v49) · [ASV](ASV.md#v49) |
| [50](BBE.md#v50) | He let his wrath have its way; he did not keep back their soul from death, but gave their life to disease. | [WEB](WEB.md#v50) · [KJV](KJV.md#v50) · [ASV](ASV.md#v50) |
| [51](BBE.md#v51) | He gave to destruction all the first sons of Egypt; the first-fruits of their strength in the tents of Ham; | [WEB](WEB.md#v51) · [KJV](KJV.md#v51) · [ASV](ASV.md#v51) |
| [52](BBE.md#v52) | But he took his people out like sheep, guiding them in the waste land like a flock. | [WEB](WEB.md#v52) · [KJV](KJV.md#v52) · [ASV](ASV.md#v52) |
| [53](BBE.md#v53) | He took them on safely so that they had no fear; but their haters were covered by the sea. | [WEB](WEB.md#v53) · [KJV](KJV.md#v53) · [ASV](ASV.md#v53) |
| [54](BBE.md#v54) | And he was their guide to his holy land, even to the mountain, which his right hand had made his; | [WEB](WEB.md#v54) · [KJV](KJV.md#v54) · [ASV](ASV.md#v54) |
| [55](BBE.md#v55) | Driving out nations before them, marking out the line of their heritage, and giving the people of Israel their tents for a resting-place. | [WEB](WEB.md#v55) · [KJV](KJV.md#v55) · [ASV](ASV.md#v55) |
| [56](BBE.md#v56) | But they were bitter against the Most High God, testing him, and not keeping his laws; | [WEB](WEB.md#v56) · [KJV](KJV.md#v56) · [ASV](ASV.md#v56) |
| [57](BBE.md#v57) | Their hearts were turned back and untrue like their fathers; they were turned to one side like a twisted bow. | [WEB](WEB.md#v57) · [KJV](KJV.md#v57) · [ASV](ASV.md#v57) |
| [58](BBE.md#v58) | They made him angry with their high places; moving him to wrath with their images. | [WEB](WEB.md#v58) · [KJV](KJV.md#v58) · [ASV](ASV.md#v58) |
| [59](BBE.md#v59) | When this came to God's ears he was very angry, and gave up Israel completely; | [WEB](WEB.md#v59) · [KJV](KJV.md#v59) · [ASV](ASV.md#v59) |
| [60](BBE.md#v60) | So that he went away from the holy place in Shiloh, the tent which he had put among men; | [WEB](WEB.md#v60) · [KJV](KJV.md#v60) · [ASV](ASV.md#v60) |
| [61](BBE.md#v61) | And he let his strength be taken prisoner, and gave his glory into the hands of his hater. | [WEB](WEB.md#v61) · [KJV](KJV.md#v61) · [ASV](ASV.md#v61) |
| [62](BBE.md#v62) | He gave his people up to the sword, and was angry with his heritage. | [WEB](WEB.md#v62) · [KJV](KJV.md#v62) · [ASV](ASV.md#v62) |
| [63](BBE.md#v63) | Their young men were burned in the fire; and their virgins were not praised in the bride-song. | [WEB](WEB.md#v63) · [KJV](KJV.md#v63) · [ASV](ASV.md#v63) |
| [64](BBE.md#v64) | Their priests were put to death by the sword, and their widows made no weeping for them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v64) · [KJV](KJV.md#v64) · [ASV](ASV.md#v64) |
| [65](BBE.md#v65) | Then was the Lord like one awaking from sleep, and like a strong man crying out because of wine. | [WEB](WEB.md#v65) · [KJV](KJV.md#v65) · [ASV](ASV.md#v65) |
| [66](BBE.md#v66) | His haters were turned back by his blows and shamed for ever. | [WEB](WEB.md#v66) · [KJV](KJV.md#v66) · [ASV](ASV.md#v66) |
| [67](BBE.md#v67) | And he put the tent of Joseph on one side, and took not the tribe of Ephraim; | [WEB](WEB.md#v67) · [KJV](KJV.md#v67) · [ASV](ASV.md#v67) |
| [68](BBE.md#v68) | But he took the tribe of Judah for himself, and the mountain of Zion, in which he had pleasure. | [WEB](WEB.md#v68) · [KJV](KJV.md#v68) · [ASV](ASV.md#v68) |
| [69](BBE.md#v69) | And he made his holy place like the high heaven, like the earth which is fixed by him for ever. | [WEB](WEB.md#v69) · [KJV](KJV.md#v69) · [ASV](ASV.md#v69) |
| [70](BBE.md#v70) | He took David to be his servant, taking him from the place of the flocks; | [WEB](WEB.md#v70) · [KJV](KJV.md#v70) · [ASV](ASV.md#v70) |
| [71](BBE.md#v71) | From looking after the sheep which were giving milk, he took him to give food to Jacob his people, and to Israel his heritage. | [WEB](WEB.md#v71) · [KJV](KJV.md#v71) · [ASV](ASV.md#v71) |
| [72](BBE.md#v72) | So he gave them food with an upright heart, guiding them by the wisdom of his hands. | [WEB](WEB.md#v72) · [KJV](KJV.md#v72) · [ASV](ASV.md#v72) |
